title: Eligibility Interviewers, Government Programs
description: Determine eligibility of persons applying to receive assistance from government programs and agency resources, such as welfare, unemployment benefits, social security, and public housing.

tasks.text: Compute and authorize amounts of assistance for programs, such as grants, monetary payments, and food stamps.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Keep records of assigned cases, and prepare required reports.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Compile, record, and evaluate personal and financial data to verify completeness and accuracy, and to determine eligibility status.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Interview and investigate applicants for public assistance to gather information pertinent to their applications.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Interview benefits recipients at specified intervals to certify their eligibility for continuing benefits.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Interpret and explain information such as eligibility requirements, application details, payment methods, and applicants' legal rights.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Initiate procedures to grant, modify, deny, or terminate assistance, or refer applicants to other agencies for assistance.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check with employers or other references to verify answers and obtain further information.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Answer applicants' questions about benefits and claim procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide social workers with pertinent information gathered during applicant interviews.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Refer applicants to job openings or to interviews with other staff, in accordance with administrative guidelines or office procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Schedule benefits claimants for adjudication interviews to address questions of eligibility.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide applicants with assistance in completing application forms, such as those for job referrals or unemployment compensation claims.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare applications and forms for applicants for such purposes as school enrollment, employment, and medical services.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Investigate claimants for the possibility of fraud or abuse.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct annual, interim, and special housing reviews and home visits to ensure conformance to regulations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Monitor the payments of benefits throughout the duration of a claim.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
